subject: docs(claude): split Code Standards out of HARD RULES into its own section
body:
HARD RULES used to carry double duty: non-negotiable runtime rules
AND code-writing standards, all flattened into one section. The
giveaway was the orphan `### REMINDER: READ AND FOLLOW THE CODE
STANDARDS EVERY TIME` at the bottom of HARD RULES — a reminder
referencing a section name that didn't exist as a top-level heading.

Split into two sibling top-level sections:

  ## HARD RULES
    - Before Making Changes
    - User Experience (CRITICAL)
    - Documentation (+ its own REMINDER, unchanged)

  ## Code Standards  (NEW)
    - Best Practices
    - Code Organization
    - Decision Documentation in Code
    - Frontend: Styles and Scripts
    - Cleanup
    - Quality Checks
    - REMINDER: READ AND FOLLOW THE CODE STANDARDS EVERY TIME

Content is byte-identical — only the parent headings and section
grouping changed. The Code Standards REMINDER now actually sits
under a Code Standards heading, matching the pattern of every
other top-level section's REMINDER line.

Placement: Code Standards sits between HARD RULES and Cross-Project
References so the prescriptive content stays grouped at the top of
the file, before project overview / architecture / configuration.

Tags: documentation, claude-md, refactor
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: communication-rules-alignment
Semver: patch
